# Psalms 44

## Verses (26)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | It has come to our ears, O God, our fathers have given us the story, of the works which you did in their days, in the old times, | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | Uprooting the nations with your hand, and planting our fathers in their place; cutting down the nations, but increasing the growth of your people. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | For they did not make the land theirs by their swords, and it was not their arms which kept them safe; but your right hand, and your arm, and the light of your face, because you had pleasure in them. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | You are my King and my God; ordering salvation for Jacob. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | Through you will we overcome our haters; by your name will they be crushed under our feet who are violent against us. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | I will not put faith in my bow, my sword will not be my salvation. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | But it is you who have been our saviour from those who were against us, and have put to shame those who had hate for us. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | Our pride is in God at all times, to his name we give praise for ever. (Selah.) | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | But now you have sent us away from you, and put us to shame; you do not go out with our armies. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | Because of this we are turned back by the attacker: those who have hate for us take our goods for themselves. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | You have made us like sheep which are taken for meat; we are put to flight among the nations. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | You let your people go for nothing; your wealth is not increased by their price. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | You have made us to be looked down on by our neighbours, we are laughed at and shamed by those who are round about us. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | Our name is a word of shame among the nations, a sign for the shaking of heads among the peoples. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | My downfall is ever before me, and I am covered with the shame of my face; | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | Because of the voice of him who says sharp and bitter words; because of the hater and him who is the instrument of punishment. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | All this has come on us, but still we have kept you in our memory; and we have not been false to your word. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | Our hearts have not gone back, and our steps have not been turned out of your way; | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | Though you have let us be crushed in the place of jackals, though we are covered with darkest shade. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | If the name of our God has gone out of our minds, or if our hands have been stretched out to a strange god, | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | Will not God make search for it? for he sees the secrets of the heart. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | Truly, because of you we are put to death every day; we are numbered like sheep for destruction. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | Why are you sleeping, O Lord? awake! and come to our help, do not give us up for ever. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | Why is your face covered, and why do you give no thought to our trouble and our cruel fate? | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | For our souls are crushed down to the dust: our bodies are stretched out on the earth. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | Up! and come to our help, and give us salvation because of your mercy. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
